payload.title: Identity Policy
payload.text:
dentity Policy — Rosetta Cards MCP
Core Principle

All artifact identity is determined solely by the canonicalized JSON payload passed to canonicalHash().

Identity must be:

deterministic

reproducible

independent of environment

immune to accidental mutation

Derived outputs (index, PNG, embeddings, etc.) must never influence identity.

Artifact Identity Rules
1. card.v1

created_at is included in the hashed payload.

It must be explicitly provided at construction time.

It must not be auto-generated inside hashing logic.

Cards represent minted artifacts (content + moment-of-mint).

Allowed inside hash:

created_at

structural content fields

Forbidden:

prototype pollution keys (__proto__, prototype, constructor)

2. event.v1

Timestamps must not contaminate identity.

occurred_at, created_at, updated_at, timestamp, time
are strictly forbidden inside the hashed payload.

Provenance/source fields are also forbidden inside the hashed payload.

These values must live in Meta sidecar artifacts.

Events represent structural occurrences, not clock-bound objects.

3. meta.v1

Meta artifacts never affect identity.

Meta is mutable.

Merge semantics: last-write-wins (LWW) unless otherwise specified.

4. summary.*.v1

Identity is derived from:

normalized inputs

sorted reference hashes

summary content

Reference order must not affect hash.

Universal Prohibitions

All artifacts must reject:

__proto__

prototype

constructor

Prototype pollution must never reach canonicalHash().

Hash Boundary Rule

canonicalHash() must only receive validated, schema-strict, artifact-approved payloads.

Artifact-specific guards must enforce:

event timestamp prohibition

proto pollution rejection

any artifact-specific exclusions

Derived Artifacts

The following must never affect identity:

PNG renders

index snapshots

embeddings

storage reports

cold archives

Derived outputs must be rebuildable from identity + meta.

Testing Requirements

All artifact types must include:

Determin
